hook.text: WhatsApp was built by two engineers who hated advertising. They charged $1 per year and reached a billion users.

buildup.text: Jan Koum grew up in Soviet Ukraine where the government monitored communications. Privacy was personal to him.

discovery.text: WhatsApp's product genius: it used your phone number as your identity. No usernames, no passwords, no friend requests.

twist.text: The team had 55 engineers serving 900 million users — the highest user-to-engineer ratio in tech history.

climax.text: Facebook bought WhatsApp for $19 billion. The product that rejected ads became the most expensive acquisition in tech.

punchline.text: No ads, no gimmicks. Just messaging that works for everyone.

quiz.question: What was WhatsApp's key product innovation?
options: Using phone numbers as identity — no usernames, passwords, or friend requests
Having the most features of any chat app
